{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the provided documents, superimposed pre-eclampsia in a pregnant female with chronic hypertension can be indicated by new onset proteinuria, low platelet count, and fresh retinal hypertensive changes. An increase in systolic BP by 30 mm Hg and diastolic by 15 mm Hg does not specifically indicate superimposed pre-eclampsia. The documents mention that new-onset severe range blood pressures (SBP ≥160 mmHg, DBP ≥110 mmHg) are indicative of preeclampsia with severe features, but an increase of 30/15 mm Hg does not necessarily meet this criterion.", "answer_choice": "C"}
